title: Building of podman v4.0.0 fails on CentOS but succeeds with v3.4.4
body:
**BUG REPORT**

/kind bug

**Description**

Starting with version 4 building from scratch fails.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Follow this guide https://podman.io/getting-started/installation#building-from-scratch for CentOS.

2. Execute `make BUILDTAGS="selinux seccomp"`

**Describe the results you received:**
```bash
# make BUILDTAGS="selinux seccomp"
Podman is being compiled without the systemd build tag.         Install libsystemd on Ubuntu or systemd-devel on rpm based      distro for journald support.
CGO_ENABLED=1 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build \
        -mod=vendor  \
        -ldflags '-X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/define.gitCommit=a429dcd2abc6aa0549e558c64ff23e1e45e49bf1 -X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/define.buildInfo=1645135335 -X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/config._installPrefix=/usr/local -X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/config._etcDir=/usr/local/etc ' \
        -tags "selinux seccomp" \
        -o bin/podman ./cmd/podman
# github.com/proglottis/gpgme
vendor/github.com/proglottis/gpgme/data.go:187:12: could not determine kind of name for C.gogpgme_data_seek
vendor/github.com/proglottis/gpgme/data.go:53:53: could not determine kind of name for C.gpgme_off_t
cgo:
gcc errors for preamble:
In file included from vendor/github.com/proglottis/gpgme/data.go:6:0:
./go_gpgme.h:15:1: error: unknown type name 'gpgme_off_t'
 extern gpgme_off_t gogpgme_data_seek(gpgme_data_t dh, gpgme_off_t offset, int whence);
 ^
./go_gpgme.h:15:55: error: unknown type name 'gpgme_off_t'
 extern gpgme_off_t gogpgme_data_seek(gpgme_data_t dh, gpgme_off_t offset, int whence);
                                                       ^

make: *** [bin/podman] Error 2
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
With version v3.4.4 it builds fine:
```bash
# make BUILDTAGS="selinux seccomp"
Podman is being compiled without the systemd build tag.         Install libsystemd on Ubuntu or systemd-devel on rpm based      distro for journald support.
CGO_ENABLED=1 \
        go build \
        -mod=vendor  \
        -ldflags '-X github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod/define.gitCommit=f6526ada1025c2e3f88745ba83b8b461ca659933 -X github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod/define.buildInfo=1645135546 -X github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod/config._installPrefix=/usr/local -X github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod/config._etcDir=/usr/local/etc ' \
        -tags "selinux seccomp" \
        -o bin/podman ./cmd/podman
CGO_ENABLED=1 \
        GOOS=linux \
        go build \
        -mod=vendor  \
        -ldflags '-X github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod/define.gitCommit=f6526ada1025c2e3f88745ba83b8b461ca659933 -X github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod/define.buildInfo=1645135616 -X github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod/config._installPrefix=/usr/local -X github.com/containers/podman/v3/libpod/config._etcDir=/usr/local/etc ' \
        -tags "remote exclude_graphdriver_btrfs btrfs_noversion exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper containers_image_openpgp" \
        -o bin/podman-remote ./cmd/podman
if [ ! -x "/usr/bin/go-md2man" ]; then \
                env GO111MODULE=off go get -u github.com/cpuguy83/go-md2man; \
fi
mkdir -p docs/build/man
```


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
CentOS 7.9.2009
conmon version 2.1.0
fuse-overlayfs: version 1.8.2
FUSE library version 3.6.1
using FUSE kernel interface version 7.29
fusermount3 version: 3.6.1
go version go1.17.7 linux/amd64

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
VirtualBox

comments.body: looks like a back dated version of gpgme-devel. We are not supporting Podman v4 on Centos 7.  either way, i dont think there is anything this team can do.

You can build Podman with the `containers_image_openpgp` build tag. Please note that creating signatures for images is not supported with this tag.

But as @baude mentions, CentOS 7 is too old for us to support it with newer versions of Podman.

I try to build Podman 4.x on Linux local and also experience similar issue. I had tried this build tag `containers_image_openpgp` and it gave me another error. What is the workaround for it?

```
Podman is being compiled without the systemd build tag. 	Install libsystemd on Ubuntu or systemd-devel on rpm based 	distro for journald support.
CGO_ENABLED=1 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build \
	-mod=vendor  \
	-ldflags '-X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/define.gitCommit= -X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/define.buildInfo=1649437502 -X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/config._installPrefix=/usr/local -X github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod/config._etcDir=/usr/local/etc ' \
	-tags "containers_image_openpgp" \
	-o bin/podman ./cmd/podman
# pkg-config --cflags  -- devmapper
Package devmapper was not found in the pkg-config search path.
Perhaps you should add the directory containing `devmapper.pc'
to the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable
No package 'devmapper' found
pkg-config: exit status 1
# github.com/containers/storage/drivers/btrfs
vendor/github.com/containers/storage/drivers/btrfs/btrfs.go:8:10: fatal error: btrfs/ioctl.h: No such file or directory
 #include <btrfs/ioctl.h>
          ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
compilation terminated.
make: *** [Makefile:316: bin/podman] Error 2
```

@jchen-9 just to eliminate the obvious things, did you follow the instructions here?  https://podman.io/getting-started/installation#building-from-scratch  Also, what command(s) did you use for the build/make?
You could also add these buildtags, (NOt sure you need both) `exclude_graphdriver_btrfs btrfs_noversion` Or install the equivalent of btrfs-prog-devel package.

> btrfs-prog-devel package

btrfs-prog**s**-devel and you're set.

Then it barfs on the gpgme132 because it uses some macros that aren't available before 170.  

So go here and get 171:  https://cbs.centos.org/kojifiles/packages/gpgme/1.7.1/0.el7.centos.1/x86_64/ 

(of course, review the source files and build process and ensure it's safe)
